request_payload.inquiry: The participant is considering quitting in the next 4 weeks due to a financial emergency involving their rented house being sold, and wants to know if they can withdraw money from their ForUsAll 401(k) plan at Check Out My LLC due to separation from employment.

response_key_points: For any future eligible termination cash withdrawal or rollover, ForUsAll procedures apply; a $75 distribution fee applies to all requests, ACH or Regular Mail Check has no additional charge, and wire or OverNight Check adds a non-refundable $35 fee.

If you take a cash distribution after becoming eligible, 20% federal withholding is mandatory, state withholding may also apply depending on state law, and the distribution is taxable income for the calendar year processed.

While still employed, in-service distributions are typically available only at age 59 1/2 or older if the plan allows; based on your birth date of 1968-05-11, that age-based route is not available to you yet.

A hardship withdrawal may be worth reviewing only if there is an immediate and serious financial need that matches an IRS-approved reason: medical expenses, purchase of a primary residence, tuition and education expenses, preventing foreclosure or eviction, funeral expenses, or repair of damage to a primary residence.

A rented-house sale does not automatically qualify as a hardship reason; if your situation involves an actual eviction risk from your primary residence, Support can help review whether the facts fit the applicable hardship category and whether the amount requested is limited to the documented need.

A 401(k) loan may also be worth reviewing while you are still active, but the plan must allow loans and participant-level checks such as vested balance, maximum number of loans, and current active loans must be confirmed.
results.response_outcome_reason: JERRY is currently Active at Check Out My LLC, has no termination date on file, and is only considering quitting in the next 4 weeks; a separation-from-employment withdrawal cannot proceed until employment has actually ended and the plan/recordkeeping status reflects that separation.
results.response_opening: JERRY, because you are currently listed as Active with Check Out My LLC and no termination date is on file, you cannot take a separation-from-employment withdrawal from your ForUsAll 401(k) right now. Your current account balance is $18,176.94, and LT Trust procedures are handled through ForUsAll.

response_steps.action: Wait until your employment has actually ended before trying to request a separation-from-employment withdrawal.
response_steps.detail: A future separation request depends on your employment status being updated after you leave Check Out My LLC.

response_steps.action: Contact ForUsAll Support if you want hardship or loan options reviewed before you separate.
response_steps.detail: You can call 844-401-2253 Monday to Friday, 7 AM to 5 PM Pacific Time, or email help@forusall.com.

response_warnings: Your latest payroll shows a loan deduction; if you have an outstanding loan when employment ends, repayment and tax consequences may apply after separation.
Do not assume a separation withdrawal, hardship withdrawal, or loan is approved until the applicable eligibility checks and plan rules are confirmed.

response_questions.question: If you want a hardship withdrawal reviewed, which IRS-approved hardship reason applies to your situation, and what amount is needed to address that expense?
response_questions.why: Hardship withdrawals require a qualifying hardship reason and the withdrawal amount cannot exceed the documented financial need.

response_questions.question: Does the sale of the rented house create an eviction situation involving your primary residence?
response_questions.why: The relevant housing hardship category in the provided rules is preventing foreclosure or eviction, and a rental sale by itself is not enough to confirm eligibility.

response_guardrails: Did not state that a separation-from-employment withdrawal can proceed while the participant is still active.
Did not say that quitting in the future makes the participant currently eligible.
Did not treat the rented-house sale as automatically qualifying for a hardship withdrawal.
Did not guarantee that the plan allows loans or that the participant qualifies for a new loan.
Did not provide tax advice or promise approval of any withdrawal, rollover, or loan.

response_escalation.reason: Support should review any possible hardship or loan alternative because the primary separation-from-employment withdrawal is blocked while JERRY remains active, and plan-specific loan availability or hardship documentation details must be confirmed.

response_data_gaps: Whether the participant's employment status will be updated after any future separation date.
Whether the plan allows loans and how many active loans are permitted.
Whether the participant currently has an outstanding loan balance, beyond the payroll loan deduction shown.
Whether the rented-house situation involves an actual eviction from the participant's primary residence.
The hardship amount needed and supporting expense details.
